title: 10 best herbal essential oils for jaw lock
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for jaw lock are a blend of natural oils that help to relax and soothe the muscles in the jaw, relieving tension and pain associated with jaw locking. These oils, including Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Cymbopogon citratus, Cinnamomum verum, Cinnamomum camphora, Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Gaultheria procumbens, and Lavandula angustifolia, have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that can ease jaw tension. If left untreated, jaw lock can lead to headaches, earaches, and difficulty eating or speaking, significantly impacting daily life and causing stress and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its calming and relaxing properties. When inhaled, lavender oil can ease muscle tension and promote a sense of calmness, which can help to release the grip of a jaw lock. The floral scent of lavender can also stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system, triggering a relaxation response that can soothe the jaw muscles and reduce clenching. By using English lavender essential oils in a diffuser or applying it topically to the temples or jawline, individuals can experience relief from jaw lock and promote overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lavender essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing tension and alleviating symptoms of jaw lock.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, making it easier to move the jaw and alleviate discomfort.

Sedative property: Lavender essential oil's sedative properties can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may be contributing to jaw lock, and promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.

Buds: The buds are chosen because they contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier in large quantities weighing 500 grams.
Dry the harvested lavender leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried lavender leaves using a clevenger apparatus and 10 liters of distilled water for 4 hours to extract essential oils.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, discarding any sediment.
Store the herbal lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for at least 24 hours before use to allow flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its natural calming properties, which can help to relax the tense muscles in the jaw and face. The herb has been used for centuries to soothe anxiety and stress, which are often contributing factors to teeth grinding and jaw clenching. When applied topically or inhaled, lemon balm oil can provide instant relief from tension headaches and facial pain associated with jaw lock, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation throughout the body.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm essential oil helps to relax and calm spasms in the muscles, which can ease tension and alleviate jaw lock caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore and inflamed muscles in the face and jaw.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm essential oil is known to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related jaw tension that may contribute to jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are rich in calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which help relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves in mid-summer for maximum oil content and quality.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves using a clean cloth or paper towels for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a glass distillation apparatus with 5-6 inches of water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil that separates from the water in a dark glass bottle and store it at room temperature for up to 12 months.
Mix 10 drops of melissa officinalis essential oil with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or jojoba oil and apply topically to the jaw area as needed three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its natural calming properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it relaxes the muscles and reduces tension in the face and jaw, providing relief from temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and grinding teeth. The soothing aroma also promotes a sense of calmness, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which can cause jaw lock.
Muscle relaxant: Chamomile's sedative properties can help relax tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing spasms and discomfort caused by jaw lock.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile essential oil has a calming effect on smooth muscle contractions, which can help relieve spasms and tension in the jaw, alleviating symptoms of jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving jaw tension and pain.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of azulene compounds, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and calm the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when dewy for maximum potency and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested flowers using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration methods within 24 hours of harvesting.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a laboratory-grade essential oil distillation apparatus with 10 parts flowers to 1 part water for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal and then decant into dark glass bottles for storage, using 15 drops of the oil per 1 tablespoon of carrier oil.
Allow the essential oil to mature and blend with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil before using it topically as a massage oil or in aromatherapy for jaw lock relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with jaw lock because it contains limonene, a compound that has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the temples or jawline, bitter orange oil can help to relax tense muscles and reduce pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. The warm, uplifting aroma of the oil can also promote relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw tension and lockjaw. As a result, bitter orange essential oils can provide natural relief from jaw lock and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bitter orange oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like limonene and alpha-pinene, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, including jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: The oil's analgesic properties, particularly from citral and linalool, can help alleviate dental pain, headaches, and facial tension that often accompany jaw lock.

Muscle relaxant: Bitter orange oil contains compounds like geraniol and neryl acetate, which may help relax tense muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, thereby reducing spasms and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils due to their rich concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to relax muscle tension and relieve jaw pain.
Buds: The buds contain a high amount of limonene, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus x aurantium peel and dry it in shade for at least 48 hours.
Blend dried peel with carrier oil in a 10% ratio of 20 grams per 200ml of oil.
Steam distill the blend using a clevenger apparatus for 2-4 hours collecting 30-40ml of essential oil.
Filter and decant the essential oil from solids using paper coffee filters and glass bottle within 24 hours.
Store the finished citrus x aurantium essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with jaw lock because it possesses a unique combination of properties that provide relief from tension and stiffness in the muscles and joints. The calming and soothing effects of lemongrass oil help to relax the masseter muscle, which is responsible for clenching and grinding, thereby reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw locks. Additionally, its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties work together to reduce pain and discomfort, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable jaw movement.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemongrass oil helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can relieve tension and discomfort associated with jaw locks. This property makes it an effective natural remedy for conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and facial muscles, making it a valuable aid in relieving jaw lock symptoms like stiffness and discomfort.

Cephalic analgesic: Lemongrass oil has natural anesthetic properties that can numb the area affected by jaw lock, providing instant relief from pain and discomfort. This property makes it an effective natural remedy for headaches, migraines, and facial pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make essential oils that help relieve jaw tension and pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and ease muscle cramps, making them effective in treating jaw lock.
Seeds: The seeds of lemongrass plant have been traditionally used to treat toothaches and gum diseases, which are often associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopopgon citratus leaves in the early morning when dew drops still cling to them weighing around 500 grams.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water in cold temperatures avoiding direct sunlight.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator set at low temperature for 24 hours or until they reach a moisture content of less than 10%.
Steam distill the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in a copper still with 300 milliliters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours collecting essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil using diatomaceous earth and store it in dark glass bottles sealed tightly at room temperature protected from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce tension and stiffness in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The oil's analgesic properties also soothe discomfort and pain, making it easier to relax the jaw muscles. Additionally, elder essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching and locking. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, elder essential oils provide natural relief from jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: Elder essential oil contains analgesic compounds that can help alleviate pain and inflammation in the jaw joint, reducing discomfort and tension caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Antioxidant property: Elder essential oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to conditions like TMJ disorder and jaw lock.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in elder essential oil may help reduce swelling and redness in the jaw joint, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an effective choice for producing essential oils that can help alleviate symptoms of jaw lock.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to relieve tension and stiffness, making it a suitable part for creating essential oils that target jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leave them to air dry for 2 weeks in a warm dark place.
Crush the dried flowers into small pieces to release their aromatic compounds.
Combine 1 part crushed flowers with 10 parts water in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Transfer the liquid infusion to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its cooling and calming properties, which can help to reduce muscle tension and spasms in the jaw. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a natural analgesic effect, numbing the pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, peppermint's aroma can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, common triggers of jaw locking and grinding. By applying topical peppermint essential oils to the affected area or inhaling it through steam inhalation, individuals can experience relief from jaw lock and TMJ discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carnosic acid: Reduces inflammation and relieves pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, providing relief from jaw tension and lock.
Menthol: Cools and relaxes the muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that can contribute to jaw lock, while also improving blood circulation and reducing swelling.
Eucalyptol: Acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory, helping to numb the pain associated with jaw lock and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds that help soothe and relax tense jaw muscles, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of peppermint contain a concentrated amount of volatile oils that can be extracted to create a potent essential oil, effective in easing jaw tension and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or a local farmer's market, collecting at least 2 cups for the extraction process.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm, dry environment for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content and enhance oil production.
Combine the dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:4 ratio, stirring well to ensure even coverage of each leaf.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 2 weeks, shaking the container daily to encourage oil release from the leaves and promoting a rich, fragrant extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the essential oil in a dark, cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, work to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles, often caused by tension and stress. When massaged into the jawline or temples, rosemary essential oils help relax tight muscles and ease the lockjaw sensation, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles, alleviating tension headaches and jaw lock caused by inflammation.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of rosemary essential oil numbs the pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock, providing relief from pain and reducing the risk of further inflammation.

Antispasmodic: Rosemary essential oil's antispasmodic property relaxes muscle spasms in the jaw, helping to release tension and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which can lead to jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax the muscles and relieve tension in the jaw.
Buds: Rosemary buds are rich in camphor, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with jaw locking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn for maximum potency and aroma then dry them completely within hours.
Crush dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder within minutes.
Steep 50 grams of powdered rosmarinus officinalis in 1 liter of cold-pressed olive oil for 4 weeks at room temperature without stirring.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and retaining essential oils within hours.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When massaged into the affected area, thyme oil can help relax the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles, reducing tension and discomfort. Additionally, its warming and soothing aroma can calm the mind and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate stress-induced jaw clenching. By addressing both physical and emotional triggers, thyme essential oils can provide effective relief from jaw lock and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Thyme essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax and soothe muscle contractions in the jaw, reducing tension and pain associated with TMJ disorders.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw and surrounding areas, which can contribute to a locked or painful jaw.
Sedative: Thyme essential oil's sedative properties can help calm nervous tension and stress, which are common triggers for bruxism (teeth grinding) and jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate muscle tension and spasms, making them effective in treating jaw lock.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content and flavor.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or air drying method within 24 hours of harvesting to preserve quality.
Crush the dried thymus vulgaris into smaller pieces and blend with carrier oils such as coconut or olive at a ratio of 1:3.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a glass container, shaking daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight and heat, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with jaw lock because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can ease tension and discomfort in the jaw muscles. The oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce pain and swelling, allowing for smoother joint mobility and reduced stiffness. Additionally, wild marjoram's soothing aroma can promote relaxation and calmness, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw locking. As a result, incorporating wild marjoram essential oils into your routine can provide welcome relief from the discomfort of a locked jaw.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram has anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and surrounding areas.
Antispasmodic: Marjoram has antispasmodic properties that may help relax the muscles in the jaw and alleviate spasms or tension that can cause jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: Marjoram contains compounds like cineole and beta-caryophyllene, which have analgesic and anesthetic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for pain relief in the jaw area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax muscles and reduce tension, making it effective for alleviating jaw lock.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, barks can help to soothe and calm the jaw area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried off the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels for 24-48 hours.
Use a coffee grinder or food processor to grind 100g of dried origanum vulgare into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered plant material with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 and steep it for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oil from the solids, then store the resulting oil in a dark glass bottle.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw lock with herbal essential oils is to blend peppermint oil and lavender oil. Massage the mixture onto the affected area to release tension and relax the muscles. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to numb the pain, while the calming properties of lavender oil soothe the mind and body. Regular application can help to reduce frequency and severity of jaw lock episodes.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw lock that are treated with herbal essential oils are migraines, tension headaches, and stress-related neck and shoulder pain. Herbs like lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus can be used in aromatherapy to relax muscle tension and ease discomfort. Additionally, blends containing ginger and frankincense may help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with these conditions.
